name: Caviar Mold
recipeIngredient: Salt and white pepper to taste
Toast rounds or unsalted crackers for serving
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle gelatin over water and let stand until softened.
2. Warm 1 cup sour cream over low heat in a saucepan. Add the softened gelatin and stir constantly until it melts, about 1 to 2 minutes. Remove from heat.
3. Add mayonnaise, lemon juice, grated onion, sugar, and hot pepper sauce to the melted gelatin mixture. Blend well to combine.
4. Place the caviar in a fine mesh strainer and rinse carefully with cold water. Shake the strainer to remove excess liquid.
5. Reserve 1 tablespoon of caviar for garnish. Add the remaining caviar to the sour cream mixture and stir gently until combined.
6. Season the mixture to taste with salt and white pepper.
7. Pour the chilled mixture into a greased 2-cup mold or divide it among 8 to 10 small individual molds.
8. Chill until firm, about 3 to 4 hours.
9. To serve, unmold the caviar onto a serving plate. Spoon about 6 tablespoons of sour cream over the large mold or approximately 1 tablespoon per each individual mold. Place some of the reserved caviar on top of the sour cream.
10. Garnish with parsley sprigs and accompany with toast rounds.
